steps.title: Identify what the hypothesis requires
steps.content: The scientists believe lava dislodged deep nitrate and released it upward. The completing evidence must show elevated nitrate near the surface in the lava-affected area, demonstrating this upwelling mechanism.

steps.title: Verify the data supports upwelling
steps.content:
for both depth ranges measured, the average concentrations of nitrate were substantially higher in the seawater in the lava-affected area than in the seawater outside of the lava-affected area. directly provides this evidence: nitrate concentrations were substantially higher in the lava-affected area than outside it at both 5–45 and 75–125 meters deep.

This shows the lava disrupted deep nutrient sources and brought nitrate to shallower depths where diatoms thrive.

The other three options either address the wrong nutrients, skip the affected vs. unaffected comparison, or describe baseline conditions.

This matches the correct answer — B is correct.
